deprecated_sources.reason: Legacy Task-3 ablation logic applies shared source-target coverage filtering, so source-missing physics descriptors are dropped and all main ablation configs collapse to the same 54-feature RDKit subset.
Stale output from the deprecated Task-3 ablation chain; nominal feature counts differ but the effective training feature set is the same across configs.
Derived from the same deprecated Task-3 ablation workflow and therefore inherits the same feature-collapse error.
Earlier exploratory repository line with divergent manuscript drafts, figure pipelines, and model-development scripts; not the authority for the current paper.
Earlier exploratory top-level script tree that should not be used as the authoritative source for current manuscript evidence without re-audit.

authoritative_sources.purpose: Corrected ablation workflow that keeps the source model on shared RDKit features while allowing target-domain physics features in the delta model.
Authoritative ablation result table for manuscript claims.
Authoritative validated candidate table; the newer version tightens confidence and decision traceability without changing DEST_eV or classification.
Script-generated manuscript metrics anchor for traceable numerical claims.
Human-readable provenance audit summarizing which worktrees and outputs are authoritative.

notes: The shared manuscript-facing round1_eval outputs compared between /home/nudt_cleng/2026/project and /home/nudt_cleng/2026/github_upload were materially consistent for the common files examined.

The difference in validated_candidates_master.csv is interpretive metadata: confidence is more conservative and decision_basis is explicit in github_upload, while DEST_eV and classification remain unchanged.
